item.title: SSSD-Extract: Dumping Active Directory Hashes from Linux Systems
item.description: Open-source tool for extracting cached AD credentials and NTLM hashes from SSSD on Linux systems. Post-exploitation for domain compromise.

item.title: HTB Machines for OSEP/PEN-300 Preparation: Complete Attack Chain Mapping
item.description: Curated list of 40+ Hack The Box machines mapped to PEN-300 exam topics with attack chains: client-side execution, AV evasion, AD exploitation, MSSQL abuse.

item.title: Dangerous PHP Functions: Code Execution and Exploitation Reference
item.description: Comprehensive catalog of PHP functions exploitable for RCE, file manipulation, and information disclosure in OSWE/AWAE assessments.

item.title: Cryptography I: Advanced Block Cipher Modes and Padding
item.description: Deep dive into CBC/CTR implementation details, IV/nonce management, padding oracle attacks, and performance comparison of encryption modes.

item.title: Cryptography I: Block Ciphers and Modes of Operation
item.description: Block cipher theory, PRP/PRF security, ECB/CBC/CTR modes, IV construction, and semantic security under chosen-plaintext attack (CPA).

item.title: OSWE/AWAE Preparation
item.description: This post contains all trainings and tutorials that could be useful for offensive security's OSWE certification.

item.title: Microcorruption CTF Tutorial: Embedded Security Reverse Engineering
item.description: Hands-on guide to MSP430 assembly debugging and memory corruption exploitation in Microcorruption CTF challenges.

item.title: Cryptography I: Stream Ciphers, PRGs, and Semantic Security
item.description: Stream cipher security, pseudo-random generators, information-theoretic security, and practical attacks on weak PRGs. Stanford Cryptography I Week 1.

item.title: About
item.description:
Background

14+ years in offensive security, red teaming, and exploit development.

I’m a security researcher focused on practical offensive techniques, vulnerability research, and building tools that solve real problems. This site documents my work and learning journey.

Professional Experience

Red Team Leader & Penetration Tester

  • 14+ years designing and executing red team operations
  • Specialized in: Active Directory attacks, post-exploitation, defense evasion
  • Experience with: Fortune 500 organizations, financial institutions
  • Tools built: SSSD-Extract, custom OSINT and exploitation frameworks

Current Focus

  • Deepening knowledge in AI/ML security through structured study
  • Exploring emerging attack vectors in enterprise systems
  • Documenting security research for the community

Certifications & Credentials

Professional certifications demonstrating expertise in offensive security:

item.title: About
item.description:
Background

13+ years in offensive security, red teaming, and exploit development.

I’m a security researcher focused on practical offensive techniques, vulnerability research, and building tools that solve real problems. This site documents my work and learning journey.

Professional Experience

Red Team Leader & Penetration Tester

  • 13+ years designing and executing red team operations
  • Specialized in: Active Directory attacks, post-exploitation, defense evasion
  • Experience with: Fortune 500 organizations, government contractors, financial institutions
  • Tools built: SSSD-Extract, custom exploitation frameworks

Current Focus

  • Deepening knowledge in AI/ML security through structured study
  • Exploring emerging attack vectors in enterprise systems
  • Documenting security research for the community

Certifications & Credentials

Professional certifications demonstrating expertise in offensive security:

item.title: Leadership & Program Design
item.description:
Red Team Program Development

Building secure systems requires understanding how attackers think. This section covers:

  • Program Design — Building risk-based adversary simulation programs
  • Team Structure — Scaling from penetration testers to red team leaders
  • Stakeholder Management — Communicating findings to executives
  • Operational Security — Running invasive assessments safely

Coming soon.

Topics in Development:

  • Building a Risk-Based Adversary Simulation Program
  • Managing Stakeholders During Invasive Operations
  • Transitioning from Pentester to Red Team Lead

item.title: Research Archive
item.description:
All Research Posts

{{ range (where site.RegularPages “Type” “posts”) }}

  • [{{ .Title }}]({{ .Permalink }}) — {{ .PublishDate.Format “January 2006” }} {{ end }}

item.title: Start Here
item.description:
Welcome to z-r0crypt

Not sure where to begin? This page guides you to the most relevant content based on your interests.

I’m Preparing for OSCP (PEN-200)

Best starting point: Foundation building, Linux privilege escalation, network penetration testing

Recommended reading order:

  1. Cryptography Fundamentals

    • Cryptography I - Week 1
    • Cryptography I - Week 2 Part 1
    • Why: OSCP includes network security fundamentals

  2. Linux Post-Exploitation

    • SSSD-Extract Tool
    • Why: Active Directory on Linux is a real attack vector

  3. Methodology & Planning
